Pipes / File Operations: These commands let you work with text files. You can download files, view parts of them, search for specific words, extract lines, and count unique words. They help analyze and process large amounts of text efficiently.  Managing Software: These commands install, update, or remove software on your system. Tools like apt-get make it easy to add applications like editors, system monitors, or web servers and cleanly remove them when not needed.  Processes / Ports: These commands let you monitor and control running programs and network ports. You can check CPU or memory usage, kill unwanted processes, run servers, and see which ports are active or in use.  Miscellaneous: These commands help with system and network checks. You can find your IP address, test connectivity with ping, perform DNS lookups, and locate installed software paths.
